name: gums
documentation: # `G`rande `U`nicast `M` ulticast `S` ender ### `gums` is multicast that just works, right out of the box. --- ## gums is designed for multicast mpegts video, but works with any multicast stream. ### Latest is v.`0`.`0`.`31` ![image](https://github.com/futzu/gums/assets/52701496/f8bfad92-2e1e-47c1-a5b2-53e5d3152e0f) ## __Install__ ```smalltalk python3 -mpip install gums ``` ### Use gums (Sender) programmatically ```py3 a@debian:~/gums$ pypy3 Python 3.9.16 (7.3.11+dfsg-2, Feb 06 2023, 16:52:03) [PyPy 7.3.11 with GCC 12.2.0] on linux Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information. >>>> from gums import GumS >>>> gummie = GumS("235.35.3.5:3535") >>>> gummie.send_stream("/home/a/mpegts/pcrvid.ts") Multicast Stream udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 Source 0.0.0.0:38835 49636512 Bytes Sent >>>> ``` ## __gums (Sender) cli__ * Supported input mpegts URIs: * files `gums -i /home/me/vid.ts` * http(s) `gums -i https://futzu.com/xaa.ts` * udp `gums -i udp://127.0.0.1:4000` * multicast `gums -i udp://@235.1.2.3:4567` * reading from stdin `cat myvideo.ts | gums` ```smalltalk usage: gums [-h] [-i INPUT] [-a ADDR] [-b BIND_ADDR] [-t TTL] [-v] optional arguments: -h, --help Show this help message and exit -i INPUT, --input INPUT Like "/home/a/vid.ts" or "udp://@235.35.3.5:3535" or "https://futzu.com/xaa.ts" -a ADDR, --addr ADDR Destination IP:Port like "227.1.3.10:4310" -b BIND_ADDR, --bind_addr BIND_ADDR Local IP to bind to like "192.168.1.34". Default is 0.0.0.0 -t TTL, --ttl TTL Multicast TTL 1 - 255 -v, --version Show version ``` #### __start gums (Sender) cli__ ```smalltalk a@debian:~/gums$ gums -i any.file stream uri: udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 a@debian:~/gums$ ``` ## __gumc (Client) cli__ ```lua usage: gumc [-h] [-i INSTUFF] [-b BYTESIZE] [-v] options: -h, --help show this help message and exit -i INSTUFF, --instuff INSTUFF default is 'udp://@235.35.3.5:3535' -b BYTESIZE, --bytesize BYTESIZE Number of bytes to read. default is to read all. -v, --version Show version ``` ### start gumc (Client) cli ```lua a@debian:~/build/clean/gums$ gumc -i udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 -b 1024 ``` ### Test gums and gumc together * first terminal, start the client, __gumc__ ```lua a@debian:~/build/clean/gums$ gumc -b 5 -i udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 ``` * second terminal,start the sender, gums__ and send a "hello" ```lua a@debian:~/build/clean/gums$ printf 'hello' | gums -a 235.35.3.5:3535 stream uri: udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 ``` ### read all bytes from multicast stream and write to file with gumc (Client) ```lua gumc -i udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 -o output.ts ``` ### read 13 bytes from a multicast stream with gumc (Client) ```lua gumc -i udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 -b 13 ``` ### read 10000 bytes from a multicast stream with gumc (Client) ```lua gumc -i udp://@235.35.3.5:3535 -b 10000 ``` ___ ___ ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/52701496/166299701-72ee908a-5053-45fc-a716-4b8ca4b1ef32.png)

modules.name: gumc
documentation: gumc, Grande Unified Multicast Client
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: gumc.version
documentation:
version prints version as a string

Odd number versions are releases.
Even number versions are testing builds between releases.

Used to set version in setup.py
and as an easy way to check which
version you have installed.

functions.name: gumc.argue
documentation: parse_args parse command line args

functions.name: gumc.show_version
documentation:
show_version shows version
if flag is set

functions.name: gumc.mk_chunksize
documentation: mk_chunksize calculates read size

functions.name: gumc.cli
documentation:
cli  makes a fully functional command line tool

usage: gumc [-h] [-i INSTUFF] [-b BYTESIZE] [-o OUTSTUFF] [-v]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -i INSTUFF, --instuff INSTUFF
                        Input, default is 'udp://@235.35.3.5:3535'
  -b BYTESIZE, --bytesize BYTESIZE
                        Number of bytes to read, default is to read all.
  -o OUTSTUFF, --outstuff OUTSTUFF
                        Output, default is sys.stdout.buffer(for piping)
  -v, --version         Show version

classes.name: gumc.GumC

methods.name: gumc.GumC.chk_bytesize
documentation:
chk_bytesize checks if self.total_bytes
is larger than self.bytesize.

methods.name: gumc.GumC.elapsed
documentation: elapsed calculates how long the client has been reading packets.

methods.name: gumc.GumC.read
documentation: read reads num_bites and returns them

methods.name: gumc.GumC.show_rate
documentation: show_rate shows read rate of gumc.

methods.name: gumc.GumC.started
documentation: started sets self.start_time if it's not set.

methods.name: gumc.GumC.write
documentation: write writes to outstuff
GumC, Grande Unified Multicast Client

modules.name: gums
documentation: gums, Grande Unicast Multicast Sender
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: gums.version
documentation: version prints version as a string

functions.name: gums.parse_args
documentation: parse_args parse command line args

functions.name: gums.fork
documentation: fork

functions.name: gums.daemonize
documentation: The Steven's double fork

functions.name: gums.cli
documentation:
cli adds command line args
passes them to a Gums instance
and calls self.send_stream
in just one function call

Use like this

import gums

if __name__ == "__main__":
    gums.cli()

classes.name: gums.GumS

methods.name: gums.GumS.is_multicast
documentation:
is_multicast tests the first byte of an ipv4 address
to see if it is in the multicast range.

methods.name: gums.GumS.mk_sock
documentation:
mk_sock makes a udp socket, self.sock
and sets a few opts.

methods.name: gums.GumS.iter_dgrams
documentation:
iter_dgrams iterates over the video and sends
self.dgram_size chunks of video to the socket.

methods.name: gums.GumS.send_stream
documentation:
send_stream sets multicast ttl if needed,
prints socket address info,
calls self.iter_dgrams,
and closes the socket
GumS class is the UDP Unicast/Multicast Sender
